title: Large File Default-Lane Demotion
decision_question: Should large_file remain eligible for the default lane at all, and if so under what exact restrictions?
hypothesis: Large-file pressure is structurally true but often low-value as an intervention signal, and should demote unless the patch directly worsened it and the repair surface is concrete.

variants.name: Demote to supporting
variants.description: Keep large_file visible only as supporting evidence under a stronger causal lead.
variants.policy_delta: Never allow large_file to occupy a primary action slot.

variants.name: Patch-local only
variants.description: Only surface large_file when the patch itself worsened reviewability in the touched file and the fix site is concrete.
variants.policy_delta: Require direct patch-local causality and fixability.

exit_bar: Confirm whether large_file improves help rate on any repo once patch-expansion cost is included.
Demote large_file from the default lane if it remains a reviewed-true but low-follow or low-help signal.
Capture an explicit keep or demote decision with the exact admissibility rule.
